From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Cabinet of the Canadian province of Saskatchewan

The **Executive Council of Saskatchewan**, often informally referred to as the **Cabinet of Saskatchewan**, is the [cabinet](/wiki/Cabinet_(government) "Cabinet (government)") of the [Canadian province](/wiki/Provinces_and_territories_of_Canada "Provinces and territories of Canada") of [Saskatchewan](/wiki/Saskatchewan "Saskatchewan").

Typically made up of members of the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") (MLAs), the Cabinet is similar in structure and role to the [Cabinet of Canada](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Canada "Cabinet of Canada"), although it is smaller in size. As federal and provincial responsibilities differ, there are a number of different portfolios between the federal and provincial governments.

The [Lieutenant Governor of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Lieutenant_Governor_of_Saskatchewan "Lieutenant Governor of Saskatchewan"), as representative of the [King in Right of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Monarchy_in_Saskatchewan "Monarchy in Saskatchewan"), formally heads the council. The lieutenant-governor does not normally attend its meetings and in practice the [Premier of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Premier_of_Saskatchewan "Premier of Saskatchewan") is its most powerful member, although its documents are often referred to as being issued by the [Governor-in-Council](/wiki/King-in-Council "King-in-Council"). Other members of the Cabinet, the [ministers](/wiki/Minister_(government) "Minister (government)"), are selected by the [Premier of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Premier_of_Saskatchewan "Premier of Saskatchewan") and appointed by the Lieutenant-Governor. Most cabinet ministers are the heads of [ministries](/wiki/Ministry_(government_department) "Ministry (government department)"), but this is not always the case.

As at the federal level, the most important Cabinet post after that of the Premier is Minister of Finance. The next most powerful position is, arguably, Minister of Health, since the Ministry has a vast budget and is of central political import. Other powerful portfolios include Justice, Education, and Energy and Resources. The government at times establishes and disbands particular ministries.

## Honorifics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Executive_Council_of_Saskatchewan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Honorifics")]

In 2019, all then-living former Executive Council members were automatically appointed honorary members of the Executive Council and styled "[The Honourable](/wiki/The_Honourable "The Honourable")" with the post-nominal letters “ECS” for life. This also applied to all current members and applies to all future members. Honorary membership is also automatically terminated upon conviction for an indictable offence.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Current Cabinet

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Executive_Council_of_Saskatchewan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Current Cabinet")]

The current ministry has been in place since 2007, when the [Saskatchewan Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Party "Saskatchewan Party") won the [general election](/wiki/2007_Saskatchewan_general_election "2007 Saskatchewan general election") of that year under the leadership of [Brad Wall](/wiki/Brad_Wall "Brad Wall"). The government was returned to office after the elections of [2011](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election") and [2016](/wiki/2016_Saskatchewan_general_election "2016 Saskatchewan general election"). On February 2, 2018, [Scott Moe](/wiki/Scott_Moe "Scott Moe") succeeded Wall as Premier, and a new cabinet was formed. Moe's government was returned to office after the elections of [2020](/wiki/2020_Saskatchewan_general_election "2020 Saskatchewan general election") and [2024](/wiki/2024_Saskatchewan_general_election "2024 Saskatchewan general election"). The current cabinet was established on November 7, 2024, after the 2024 general election.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Members are listed in [order of precedence](/wiki/Order_of_precedence_in_Saskatchewan "Order of precedence in Saskatchewan").

| Deputy Premier       & Minister of Finance       & Minister of Immigration and Careers Training       & Minister of Labour Relations and Workplace Safety | [Jim Reiter](/wiki/Jim_Reiter "Jim Reiter") | 2009–present | 2024–present |

| Minister of Agriculture | [Daryl Harrison](/wiki/Daryl_Harrison "Daryl Harrison") | 2024–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Social Services | [Terry Jenson](/wiki/Terry_Jenson "Terry Jenson") | 2024–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Energy and Resources | [Colleen Young](/wiki/Colleen_Young_(politician) "Colleen Young (politician)") | 2024–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Trade and Export Development       & Minister Responsible for Innovation | [Warren Kaeding](/wiki/Warren_Kaeding "Warren Kaeding") | 2018–2022; 2024–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Government Relations       & Minister Responsible for First Nations, Métis and Northern Affairs       & Minister Responsible for the Provincial Capital Commission | [Eric Schmalz](/wiki/Eric_Schmalz "Eric Schmalz") | 2024–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Parks, Culture and Sport       & Minister Responsible for the Status of Women       & Minister Responsible for Tourism Saskatchewan       & Minister Responsible for [Saskatchewan Liquor and Gaming Authority](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Liquor_and_Gaming_Authority "Saskatchewan Liquor and Gaming Authority") | [Alana Ross](/wiki/Alana_Ross "Alana Ross") | 2024–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Mental Health and Addictions, Seniors and Rural and Remote Health | [Lori Carr](/wiki/Lori_Carr "Lori Carr") | 2018–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Highways       & Minister of SaskBuilds and Procurement       & Minister Responsible for the [Global Transportation Hub Authority](/wiki/Global_Transportation_Hub_Authority "Global Transportation Hub Authority") | [Dave Marit](/wiki/Dave_Marit "Dave Marit") | 2016–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Environment | [Travis Keisig](/wiki/Travis_Keisig "Travis Keisig") | 2024–present | 2024–present |
| Minister of Advanced Education | [Ken Cheveldayoff](/wiki/Ken_Cheveldayoff "Ken Cheveldayoff") | 2007–2020; 2024–present | 2024–present |
